rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or declared as helpers in the same annex.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning helper declarations with the types of their expressions, e.g., bool for comparisons, int for integers.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are valid and visible in the current scope.
Validate that expression types match declared helper types to prevent '<error>' type issues.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid annex scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
Use only AADL features or helper declarations that are visible in the same annex to avoid resolution errors.
anti_patterns: Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the model's configured dependencies.
Do not add architecture elements to fix unresolved references; instead, correct the root cause or remove invalid clauses.
